5th-strata/corona.html.title: Corona

cataclysms.html.title: Cataclysms
cataclysms.html.description:
The realms lying between the Multiverse and the Exoverse simultaneously, major changes and events between the two ripples the wall, causing changes within a seemingly random subset of the population.These events, as far as the denizens of the realms are concerned, are random. And they are catastrophic. The entire realm shakes as if suffering from an ever increasingly cataclysmic earthquake, winds tear through the landscapes via horrific tornados, the central hive gains a blind bloodlust trying to slaughter every living thing each individual encounters, including themselves, and geomagnetic storms, infused with mana, rock what little technology the denizens utilize.C is equal to the number cataclysms the realms have experienced, counting the one they are currently experiencing.These events have become more violent with each series of ripples, enough so that the sliver hives are beginning to take close to a year to recover from the damages to the environments and their internal hive structures.
Creation of Point Prime
When the first Supervoid was born in the exoverse, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the population became monochrome, their hearts became replaced with magical singularities, they became biologically immortal, and they gained the power to teleport around their home realms. They consume a fraction of the 'light' the souls, of those who dies near them. A wave of gravity that minorly harms the health of those nearby, yet rejuvenates the one who emitted it, comes from their heart when they do so. These beings seem to have difficulties increasing their numbers compared to normal Humans. Sotheran Collapse
When the thousandth Supervoid was born in the exoverse, it rippled across the realms. Those who were previously changed changed further, gaining the ability to reinforce their own matter with the matter of another who is willing, becoming stronger and tougher beyond what many could even imagine. Time Spiral
When the past of Dominaria crashed into it's present simultaneously, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans had their hair change into a nebulae-esque material, while their irises became starfields. When these individuals died, they remained, their spirit becoming an entity as real and tangible as they were when alive, but altered. No longer needing to eat, drink, breath, or sleep, born with an overriding emotion, and becoming much much harder to kill. Planar Chaos
When the adjacent presents of Dominaria coexisted together, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans began to cast multiple shadows in the presence of light. They gained the ability to temporarily split themselves into multiple variations of themselves, each real and physical, taking actions that the individual considered to take simultaneously. Then, all but one present collapses back into one. Future Sight
When the possible futures of Dominaria washed over the present, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans had their eyes glow with a strange source less glimmer. They were each able to see potential futures as shadowy echoes of what could be. With some minor focus, they can see only the most likely futures. With extreme skill and training, they have the ability to see what will happen rather than what may happen. The Great Mending
When planeswalkers were reduced from gods to merely powerful wizards, it rippled across the realms. The efficacy of cosmium infusions degraded significantly. Eventide, Morningtide, Eclipsed
When the balance of the Great Aurora was returned, it rippled across the realms. A portion of the humans had their hair change color to that of their home realm. These humans gained minor arcane powers and slightly enhanced physicality during the day, at night, their magic and physicality would become elevated by magnitudes as they became werewolves, while away from the day or night skies, they became a somewhat weaker version of a typical human. Fraught with conflicting psychologies, weak constitutions, and generally erratic behavior. These 'eclipsed' versions grow fangs, claws, and wolf-like ears. The Great Pruning
When planeswalkers were deprived of their sparks reducing many of them to normal wizards, it rippled across the realms. The efficacy of cosmium infusions was further reduced. Reality Fracture
When Jace attempted to right all wrongs creating alternate possibilities, a portion of the humans became replaced. They were fractured into Splinters, aware of an alternate history and reality than what they are currently experiencing. Similar in disposition to the human they replaced, even similar names, but undoubtedly a different individual altogether.

engine.html.title: Engine
engine.html.description:
The Planar Portal that enables travel to the multiverse and [other realms](War Web).
The Eternity Column that enables travel to the exoverse and the other realms (if you know the location of the others).
It coordinates the leylines from both realities towards the center, funnels souls into itself, it facilitates the stabilization of the chaos wall into something substantially real, the expansion of the realms, and the drive of the sliver hives to protect it.The engine itself is often underground, a massive sphere of liquid silver in another spherical cave. What little space between the engine and the cave is a torrent of energy, mana, and dust. A maelstrom of wind cycling the energy around the entire space and into the liquid metal. The whispers of the dead can be sometimes heard in the wind, the windswept cave walls are pockmarked with holes, that lead to the sliver hive proper, the ambient mana flowing into the space plentiful enough to generate not only light but also heat.
In every realm, save Corona, a single drop of silvery metal with fall from the engine every certain period of time. On the floor of this cave, it will congeal and coalesce into a Thrum, which is forcefully integrated into the realm's hive by the engine above.The amount of time between each droplet of liquid metal can be found via the following equation.Meaning, currently, a thrum is spawned every sixty two minutes and thirty two and a half seconds.In Corona, the engine has fractured and split into many pieces, each contributes the the chaotic expansion of the realm and all but one of these engines spawns a Thrum at different rates. The central engine, the one 'core' of Corona does not spawn any.

human.html.title: Human
human.html.description:
"The old stories claim that our ancestors conquered worlds, they bled a star to vanquish the dark, they fought that which extinguished the light. We served gods who some believe abandoned us...what is it that you believe?" - UnknownHumans were once Auxilary forces for the Fomori in their multiple wars. Notably serving as Foreign Legions against the Eldrazi of the Exoverse, and as Penal Legions against the Vampires of Ixalan. Though, due to time and the Cataclysms, their once rigid militarism has given way to multiple different cultures, shaped by the mana of their home. They have, generally, retained working amounts of Exosuits, Managuns, Lightblades, and knowledge of Cosmium infusions onto their own persons.For some reason, those affected by the Cataclyms do not usually seek to use or make these artifacts for some reason.
Heirlooms from before can include armor, fueled by Moxite, granting them extremely enhanced physical abilities. Mechanically, it grants +4/+0, absorb 2, and ward {2} to the equipped creature.

Ironclad Revolutionary by Eric Deschamps
Some Heirlooms from before can include weapons made out of Etherium, mana guns and lightblades. The Lance, a handheld weapon, and the rifle, a two handed heaver variant.Lances condense the ambient mana into a bolt and acts as a sort of railgun for the bolt. It takes a number of seconds to charge a single shot equal to the number of the realm's colors plus one. Effectively, it deals 1 damage to target creature as a tap effect.

Kellan, the Kid by Magali Villeneuve
Rifles use mana crystals to overcharge the power into a potent spell, with a cooldown of triangular(X) * .5 seconds (.5 seconds for a crystal from Komas, 10.5 seconds for a crystal from Corona). Each mana crystal can sustain sixty five shots. X is equal to the number of colors of the mana crystal plus 1. Mechanically, equipped creature attacks to cast a copy of the spell with X as specified.

Taii Wakeen, Perfect Shot by David Auden Nash
Lightblades are hilts, polearms, or gauntlets that emit a constant blade of mana. Each color has their own 'motif' for the type of weapon their lightblades tend to manifest as. White manifests swords, blue manifests spears, black manifests curved blades such as scythes, sickles, and khopeshes, red manifests axes, and green manifests as claws or forearm mounted weaponry. Komas lacks lightblades, the ambient mana being too thin for form a blade in the first place. Mechanically, lightblades give +1/+0, enter attached to a creature, and gives that creature lifelink, first strike, deathtouch, trample, and/or haste until the end of the turn it entered. The keywords dependent on the colors of the lightblade.

Honored Knight-Captain by Forrest Imel

Treasure Hunt by Steve Prescott

Scourge for Eternity by Konstantin Porubov

Memorial Team Leader by Andrew Mar

Red Tiger Mechan by Simon Dominic
They have a tradition of using Cosmium to give themselves tattoos that glow an eldritch purplish white. They used to be more effective in the power they granted, but over the course of two specific Cataclysms, they have become but glimmers of their prior selves. The ratio to surface area and power gained is now, considerably, abysmal.Below is what they were originally able to use, now, the smallest two areas and the last two spells are no longer accessible to them. Atleast, the two smallest areas are not noticeably impactful in effect. Effectively, the spells shifted up the area required to cast it by two. Each tattoo correlates to a single cast of a single spell a day (Beginning of upkeep, creature becomes prepared). The color identity of the realm determines the color spells they can have access to.

Braided Net // Braided Quipu by Diego Gisbert

slivers.html.title: Slivers
slivers.html.description:
Slivers have four methods of reproduction, one from the multiverse, one from the exoverse, one unique to these [pocket realms](War Web), and one shared between the multiverse and exoverse.
The first, unique to these spaces, is autogenesis. The liquid metal pool in the heart of every hive that surrounds the Engine, periodically spawns thrum. The number spawned has increased over the course of centuries and millennia, initially having been one at what we would call 'midnight'.

Thrumming Hivepool by Rob ReyThe second, the multiversal method, is transfiguration. Non-shapeshifters who find themselves within the Hivelands will find themselves slowly transformed into Slivers themselves. This process is extremely long, but takes exponentially less time the closer one is to the Engine. Going from effectively centuries to mere minutes. Proximity to the Engine is the only factor that changes the rate of transformations, but they can cause physiological and psychological issues in the victim of the process. Issues which can result in death, disabilities, and/or insanity. The quicker the process is, the more painful it is to endure. It is this factor that has prevented successful excursions into the hivelands to shut down, take control of, or otherwise modify the Engines of the realms.

Metamorphose by Ron SpencerThe third, the exoversal method, is oviparity. Specifically, utilizing externally fertilized parasitic ovipositors. Basically, xenomorphing people. Stabbing them with a specific tendrils that can extend from their beak-like 'faces' and implanting an embryo. It uses the genetics of the host to determine the color alignment and future adaptation (if any), technically the external fertilization is this process of utilizing the host's dna. If you are, at the time of being implanted, classified as a blue black being, then the sliver who emerges would either be colorless or blue black. The host will be unconscious for a day, due to adjacently injected toxins that quells the immune system and causes the host to believe they simply fell asleep rather than having been injected with an embryo, then it will develop over the course of ten days in the host, then spend five days after violently bursting from the host's torso to mature fully into a Thrum or fully adapted Sliver.

Syphon Sliver by Johann Bodin
The fourth, the shared method, is parthenogenesis. Slivers are capable of laying eggs via asexual reproduction. This can only be done while the individual's cortisol levels are low to non-existent, otherwise known as the individual feeling safe and secure. There are, of course, exceptions or modifications to this if the hiveform is specialized for reproduction in some capacity. Three notable examples being Sliver Queen, The First Sliver, and Hatchery Sliver. The queen can simply do this regardless of state and hatchery overdrives this process.

Hatchery Sliver by Svetlin Velinov

splinter.html.title: Splinter
splinter.html.description:
Splinters are odd humanoids, highly social, yet oddly solitary. They are descendants of Slivers that 'evolved individualism' as best they can figure, around the time the Fomori disappeared, in an alternate reality where Humans were never left behind. Overtime, they have lost a majority of their 'sliveriness', they barely have a hivemind, they are barely shapeshifters, and they are humanoid in form. They often regard the sliver hive in a similar manner to a human and a chimpanzee, not the same and not the same level of sentience/sapience generally, yet something that can definitely be dangerous if provoked.
On the hivemind point, they have a thought-weft. They are able to telepathically communicate with one another and share emotions, but not share their thoughts, memories, or experiences. It also lends them a general sense of the direction and distance of the nearest sliver, which being no longer in the hive, they avoid. As well as a far more accurate sense of the direction and distance of other splinters.On the humanoid point, beyond that, they look off. Their bodies are covered in chitinous plates (grey and marbled by the realm's colors), their eyes are more akin to gemstones that are the same color of the realm's colors mixed together, they have "hair" that is more like the squirming tentacles of a jellyfish or polyp than true hair, and they have cloaks of leathery flesh that is oddly similar to tanned human skin when at rest. They do not have ears or mouths that are visible, rather they seem to have plates around such structures, either protecting those structures or giving the illusion that such structures exist. Their humanoid appearance is assumed to be a subconscious adaptation to put the other sapients at ease, the cloak of flesh and their mouth/ear guards to mimic clothes. Compared to normal slivers, these adaptations were made over the course of millennia rather than moments. As a species, they have witnessed the other races arise and have cemented themselves as an enigmatic, yet benevolent, species within the collective racial memories of the other races.On the shapeshifter point, what little they can shapeshift depends drastically upon the realm they inhabit, though one trait they all share is the ability to change the texture and coloration of the outside of their fleshy cloaks much like an octopus can change their skin's texture and color.

war-web.html.title: War Web
war-web.html.description:
During the Fomori-Eldrazi War, the Fomori of the Multiverse travelled across the chaos wall frequently. To facilitate the supply lines for this war, they built staging grounds into the chaos wall, spaces between the finite infinity between the multiverse and the edge.
These pockets stabilized the chaos, concentrated it into mana, and then concentrated it into matter. A self perpetuating Engine of expansion that served as staging grounds, relay stations, and recharging stations. Each one aligned with different colors of mana to serve as perfect 'batteries' for different machines and purposes.
The Fomori had many war machines, but with the war against the Eldrazi and Ixalan both going simultaneously, they implemented an unusual force to safeguard these locations from the near constant invasions of the Eldrazi. They used a peculiar organism they found within both the edge and the multiverse, Slivers.
Making artifacts to control their hiveminds, they were able to have the Slivers reliably safeguard the planar portals and eternity columns that were built into the center of these pockets, allowing themselves, their ships, and their war machines to pass through uncontested, while aggressively fighting and adapting to the Eldrazi menace. A strategy not unlike the methods employed by Volrath to guard the Legacy, but on a more grand scale.The Fomori did not come into these pockets entirely devoid of other forms of life. Bringing in the slivers and coming in themselves, they had various hitchhikers, ancestors of the flora and fauna that can be found in the pockets today. Though, as they were not intentionally seeded with life nor designed to host life other than Slivers, major forms of life are few and not particularly diverse.
Since the Fomori disappeared, the pockets expanded, life continued to adapt, and the concentrations of mana warped the landscape. As they expanded, they weakened the wall in specific nebulous points, which has facilitated the crossing between the two occasionally. Such as Tezzeret entering into the Edge.
Various unintended side effects of these pockets have become prominent over the millennia, leylines from the Edge slowly grows Moxite along them, while leylines from the Multiverse slowly grows Etherium along them. They act as a sort of 'self-repairing' 'natural' wire that conducts the mana into the central 'engine'. Overtime, the denizens of the realms have mined and harvested the materials, which has resulted in a higher concentration in the center of the realms, where the leylines converge and feed into the 'engine'.
The souls of the Slivers, along with the various modifications they made to their biology, were designed to use the leylines to feed into the 'engine'. The souls of everything else, was not. The dead have their souls shredded, mere remnants at best remain, while the rest travels along the leylines. The edges, emotions, memories, non-sliver form/identity, all snag along the leylines, forming and growing cosmium deposits along them. Unlike the veins/wires of metal that grow from the outside inwards, these veins grow from the inside outwards.Due to the constant invasions of Eldrazi and the hyper territorial Slivers, major settlements have never appeared on any of the realms. There are small pockets of society on many of the realms, but that is just about all there is in nations.
These realms were staging grounds for military forces, those forces were Human auxilaries, foreign legions, and/or penal legions. Outfitted with exosuits fueled by moxite, mana guns and light blades made of etherium, and various modifications from cosmium infusions. They were left here when the Fomori disappeared, and it wasn't long before the slivers began to view them as threats and shoved them to the outskirts of the realms. Those that remain have maintained only a shadow of their past glories, with Cataclysms, major catastrophes from the multiverse and exoverse, having modified random fractions of their populations, creating further distinct sub populations.
The most recent cataclysm has resulted in the arrival of Splinters, beings who had evolved independence in a version of the realms where humans never were.Oddly, Eldrazi incursions have become less frequent in the past decade or so. Similarly, Sliver attacks against Remnants travelling through the Hivelands have become more frequent.Despite each realm being objectively flat, a half-sphere of land and atmosphere, there is a visual illusion, perhaps induced by magic, perhaps some sort of gravitational distortion, that causes the realms to visually curve at roughly a rate of 0.009 degrees per kilometer to those on them.(For the purposes of anyone who looks into patterns, each integer is capable of being multiplied by 10 kilometers, starting where the prior ring ended and being that many km thick.)
(s is the number of legendary slivers in WUBRG there are, x is the number of legendary slivers in the color/hive, n is the number of unique arts for the legendary slivers in WUBRG (minus 1 for legion (crayon) and plus 1 for queen (duelmasters), L is the number of locus there are)Each realm subtly rotates within the chaos wall, facing the chaos wall twice in a rotation, they experience day as the exosphere shines against a blank background, creating a near 'source less flat light' upon everything. Facing the multiverse or exoverse, they experience night, the starfields of the night skies being the planes and planetary systems of the multiverse and exoverse respectively. A full rotation lasts twenty five hours, each day and night lasting six hours and fifteen minutes exactly.
On the horizon, beyond the skies, through the first two layers of the Exospheres lies a basic translation of the blind eternities into something real and definable. Shadows dance across the skies through this translation, some quick, others languid, but all are the forms of Eldrazi within the Chaos Wall.Often, these shadows will move into the exosphere and result in an eldrazi drone or two. This occurs frequently enough that the realmic denizens are unaware of the fact that Eldrazi are not normal forms of fauna.The rest of the non-sapient life are descendants of worms, faeries, insects, germs, gremlins, pests, and fungi in some manner. The tagalongs, ride alongs, and parasites that were on the ships of the fomori who stopped upon the realms.The center of each realm, save Corona, lies the engine surrounded by a sliver hive and the territory that hive has claimed. This territory is patrolled consistently enough by slivers that it is near impossible for any non-sliver faunal life to survive there, as they would be swarmed expeditiously by the hive. Splinters are the only ones brave enough to risk entering the territories.The Hivelands are large, usually preventing direct travel from one end of the realm to the other, instead, having to go around them, a journey that can take quite some time depending on the realm.The band around the hivelands, sandwiched between it and the Exosphere, is the area where the rest of the realm's matter exists. It is not rigorously patrolled by the local hive, but they do venture out into the areas occasionally.The edges of these lands are often consumed by Eldrazi or are desolate.When making it to the absolute edge of the realm, it simply drops off into the exosphere. The edge glowing with a silvery light as matter accumulates.The Exosphere is composed of three distinct spheres.The first sphere, the Glimmervoid, like a fog of silvery dust, translates mana into matter and accumulates it into the realm. Stone on stone, air on air, water on water, etc. It also serves to recycle matter that enters into it as material deposited into the realms.The second sphere, the Mana Confluence, is nothing but light, heat, and mana. This sphere translates the aether from the blind eternities into mana and energy. It slowly becomes converted into the realm's colors and serves as the generalized light source of each realm's daytime.The third and final sphere, the Echoes of Eternity, a shadowy scape that can drive those who look upon it mad. Shadows of the true forms of eldrazi move within, glimpses of the exosphere and multiverse can be seen through it, and the destructive aether that annihilates matter infusing it all. This sphere somehow translating the blind eternities of the chaos wall into a definable tangible reality.The more colors of mana that needs to be processed or manufactured, the less stable the process is and the exponentially larger the exosphere needs to be.With the exception of Corona, which is an aberration in how it is structured, the following equations can be used to determine the sizes of each of the realms. X being the number of lands there are after the sliver hive and Y being the number of colors the realm is. The result is in kilometers and includes the exosphere.The bottom of the multiverse lies a single realm, that of no color.
Komas - A realm devoid of colors of mana, unique for its mechanical Slivers. The first strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess only a single color of mana.
Lumos - A realm of white mana
Souse - A realm of blue mana
Nocturne - A realm of black mana
Ignis - A realm of red mana
Sett - A realm of green mana The second strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess a pair of colors of mana.
Unnamed 1 - A realm of wu mana
Ioxis - A realm of wb mana
Unnamed 2 - A realm of wr mana
Iethol - A realm of wg mana
Noxou - A realm of ub mana
Zyxkal - A realm of ur mana
Unnamed 3 - A realm of ug mana
Montresor - A realm of br mana
Unnamed 4 - A realm of bg mana
Unnamed 5 - A realm of rg mana The third strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities possess three colors of mana.
Hytropo - A realm of wub
Unnamed 6 - A realm of wur
Unnamed 7 - A realm of wug
Unnamed 8 - A realm of wbr
Unnamed 9 - A realm of wbg
Unnamed 10 - A realm of wrg
Unnamed 11 - A realm of ubr
Unnamed 12 - A realm of ubg
Unnamed 13 - A realm of urg
Kithestra - A realm of brg The fourth strata of the chaos wall's holes are the realms who's identities lack a single color of mana.
Unnamed 14 - A realm of wubr
Unnamed 15 - A realm of wubg
Unnamed 16 - A realm of wurg
Unnamed 17 - A realm of wbrg
Unnamed 18 - A realm of ubrg The top of the multiverse lies a singular realm who's identity is all encompassing.Corona, being the aberration it is, uses a different equation for it's size.
Corona - A realm of wubrg

info_defaultFileIcon.description: The icon to use for files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_defaultMediaIcon.description: The icon to use for media files. Prefix with 'lucide//' to use a Lucide icon

info_showOrphanNodes.description: Show nodes that are not connected to any other nodes.

info_showAttachments.description: Show attachments like images and PDFs as nodes in the graph.

info_allowGlobalGraph.description: Allow the user to view the global graph of all nodes.

info_allowExpand.description: Allow the user to pop-out the graph view to take up the whole screen

info_attractionForce.description: How much should linked nodes attract each other? This will make the graph appear more clustered.

info_linkLength.description: How long should the links between nodes be? The shorter the links the more connected nodes will cluster together.

info_repulsionForce.description: How much should nodes repel each other? This will make disconnected parts more spread out.

info_centralForce.description: How much should nodes be attracted to the center? This will make the graph appear more dense and circular.

info_edgePruning.description: Edges with a length above this threshold will not be rendered, however they will still contribute to the simulation. This can help large tangled graphs look more organised. Hovering over a node will still display these links.

info_minNodeRadius.description: How small should the smallest nodes be? The smaller a node is the less it will attract other nodes.

info_maxNodeRadius.description: How large should the largest nodes be? Nodes are sized by how many links they have. The larger a node is the more it will attract other nodes. This can be used to create a good grouping around the most important nodes.

info_allowResizing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be resized

info_allowCollapsing.description: Whether or not to allow the sidebars to be collapsed

info_rightDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the right sidebar

info_leftDefaultWidth.description: The default width of the left sidebar

info_selector.description: CSS selector for an element. The feature will be placed relative to this element.

info_type.description: Will this feature be placed before, after, or inside (at the beggining or end).

info_featurePlacement.description: Where to place this feature on the page. (Relative to the selector)

info_sourcePath.description: The local path to the source .html file which will be included.

info_allowFoldingLists.description: Whether or not to allow lists to be folded

info_allowFoldingHeadings.description: Whether or not to allow headings to be folded

info_siteUrl.description: The url that this site will be hosted at

info_authorName.description: The name of the author of the site

bodyClasses: publish css-settings-manager styled-scrollbars show-inline-title show-ribbon is-focused
